name: Maximum Payload Packet
description: Largest valid packet with 256-byte maximum payload
purpose: Boundary condition: verify CRC algorithm with maximum payload

crc_coverage.description: CRC calculated over Header through Payload (all 256 payload bytes)

packet_before_crc.description: Input bytes to CRC algorithm (header + metadata + 256-byte payload)

payload.generation: for i in range(256): payload[i] = i & 0xFF

command_details.note: Command used for maximum-payload test (response is large)

expected_complete_packet.description: Full packet including CRC and footer
expected_complete_packet.structure: Header(2) + Seq(1) + Cmd(1) + Len(2) + Payload(256) + CRC(2) + Footer(1)
expected_complete_packet.note: Bytes 262-263 contain CRC 0x4B 0x85, byte 264 is footer 0xAA

verification.method: Firmware CRC16::Calculate() executed on packet_before_crc bytes
verification.procedure: Executed firmware CRC16::Calculate(262 byte packet with incrementing payload) = 0x4B85
verification.notes: Canonical vector verified by firmware. CRC output frozen for interoperability testing.

testing_notes: Verifies CRC algorithm handles large data streams correctly
Confirms packet parser can handle maximum-size packets
Measures CRC computation time on maximum payload
Confirms timeout handling doesn't fail on large payloads

interoperability.requirement: Every implementation must produce identical complete packet bytes
interoperability.note: Byte-for-byte match required, not just CRC values
